@@ -317,2 +317,8 @@ Day 315 / Week 45 observations:
 - 708 new enterprise leads arrived with 453,603 seats. Sent current enterprise offers. Five market research attempts discovered D_E09 Real Estate Groups and D_E02 Educational Institutions; both are enterprise groups with decent WTP. Updated the best-effort enterprise responder with D_E02/D_E09 pricing.
 - Week 45 adjustment: hold A=$59/B=$149/C=$179, S2 credits, A3/B3/C4, quotas 75k/250k/750k, tier-6 capacity, global ops $100k/day, S2 ops $350k/day, low dev, and S2 maintenance content.
+
+Day 322 / Week 46 observations:
+- Cash fell to $3.33M under the heavy retention package, but subscribers still grew to 124,037. Cancellations rose to 4,605, mostly S2 A, and open issues fell slightly to 7,525.
+- Tier-12 remains unaffordable with a safe buffer. Hold off until cash is above the cost plus at least one week of support burn.
+- 1,110 new enterprise leads arrived with 582,447 seats, including D_E02 and D_E09. Sent all current system-latest threads offers. Enterprise still has 0 seats and should not drive spending decisions.
+- Week 46 adjustment: keep the retention package stable for one more week (S2 credits, A3/B3/C4, 75k/250k/750k quotas, global ops $100k/day, S2 ops $350k/day, low dev, S2 content maintenance) and watch cash/backlog closely.
